title: Stelis batillacea
taxonomy: Stelis batillacea belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. This orchid species is a part of the genus Stelis, which is a large and complex group within the orchid family. The genus Stelis is further divided into various subgenera and sections, but the exact classification of Stelis batillacea may vary depending on the source. Within the Orchidaceae family, Stelis batillacea is classified as a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows in the ground rather than on other plants. The exact classification of Stelis batillacea is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany.

intro: Stelis batillacea, also known as 'Bat's Wing Orchid', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis batillacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis batillacea include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory conditions and as a natural remedy for sore throats and fever.
